feedback.text: Dear Parlimentary members. Imagine you having a freezer/fridge, and it works fine. Light lights up when you open it. It keeps everything nice and cool. It's not noisy. It draws A+ power. It's even in your favorite colour. Then suddenly, the factory who makes your freezer/fridge, decides they no longer want to make that. And suddenly your freezer/fridge is dead. You can't even open the door. It's literally locked from the inside. This is what happens to games. When a publisher decides it's done with a game, they stop service. Which is fine. But they left all the little codes that REQUIRE online connection with that publisher to be available at all times. Either via a launcher outside of the game. Or some DRM/Anticheat measure. The license we purchased, are null and voided. The game we purchased maybe 3 days before, gone. Another example, much more obvious I guess. Your car. Much of it is computerized. From Satnav, to ECM in the motor, to the car itself, like a Tesla, whatever you may feel about it's producer, all electronic, and able to work and run smoothly, as long as it's being provided a server from the manufacturer. And you're driving to your kids school to deliver some goodies for the class. Then your car stops. Not even pulling over to the side. Doors locked. No ignition. Not even the cars hazard lights come on. That is a REAL problem with the digital electric cars. It is what is happening to games. We, the costumers, have been pushed from owning the game, via physical media, to digital, and somewhere on that road, we stopped owning the product we bought for 60 Euro. To holding a digital copy of a license. A license that are worthless as a license. As it can be rewoked, nullified, and teared into pieces, at the whim of a CEO. We are no longer a customer. We're consumer cattle, only good to be milked for every cent, and then shot on the spot, when the farmer don't wanna milk anymore. No pastures to live out our days, no stable to huddle in for cozy warmth and conformt. As the farmer (publisher) decided to lock all the doors, bar the gates, and let the farm fall into ruin. All of this can be stopped easily. The publisher simply need to implement a simple code to stop the game from pinging home for running. In terms of multiplayer games, it's equally simple. Release the tools to make servers. They have them. They are done using them. They don't want to use them ever again. If you don't want to do it for me. And the millions of gamers out there today. Do it for your kid, who will not be able to play his childhood games he loved. Or that you loved. Remember Nintendo will sue people who emulate games, they long since buried. We are not allowed to play stuff we have bought. Even when we actually bought the game. And not just the worthless license. Kindest regards Simon Sørensen, Danish citizen and gamer since 1978.
